The natives are much more numerous in some parts of Australia than they are in others, but nowhere is the country thickly peopled ; some dire disease occasionally breaks out among the natives, and carries off large numbers. This was the case among the Goulbura, Devil's River, and Upper and Lower Murray tribes some few years before the country was peopled by the whites ; — the small-pox, or some very similar disease, made its appearance, and played havoc among the tribes.
